title: podman run port forward range: ncat: connection refused
body:
```
[+1234s] not ok 512 [500] podman run port forward range
...
<+015ms> # # podman-remote --url unix:///var/tmp/podman_tmp_YncV exec -d c95eaa0d5b1359c76cb1f267a9c3ba726564b0414b34122723c631b38e0d1c7a nc -l -p 5866 -e /bin/cat
<+090ms> # c0a0d47f72158daa1fa8d61cc9882ca40623d31ceabee77f0ea4d0bf822238b8
         # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
         # #|     FAIL: ncat got data back (netmode=bridge port=5866)
         # #| expected: '9PD2bgUX1v'
         # #|   actual: 'Ncat: Connection refused.'
         # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```
Only remote, and only f38. "Oh, f38, this is a CNI bug, and we don't care". Nope - still happening even after we bumped f38 VMs to netavark.


* fedora-38 : sys remote fedora-38 root host boltdb [remote]
  * PR #21512
    * [02-05 08:54](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6187533038518272/html/sys-remote-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00512) in [sys] [500] podman run port forward range
  * PR #21431
    * [02-06 06:45](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4753038120845312/html/sys-remote-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00512) in [sys] [500] podman run port forward range
  * PR #21413
    * [02-02 15:44](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6600965382471680/html/sys-remote-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00512) in [sys] [500] podman run port forward range
  * PR #21384
    * [01-31 10:08](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5499119775514624/html/sys-remote-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00511) in [sys] [500] podman run port forward range
  * PR #21366
    * [01-27 08:45](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6434932650672128/html/sys-remote-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00511) in [sys] [500] podman run port forward range
  * PR #17831
    * [02-06 11:13](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4634216978186240/html/sys-remote-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00512) in [sys] [500] podman run port forward range
    * [01-31 11:32](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5084308143079424/html/sys-remote-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00511) in [sys] [500] podman run port forward range

| sys(7) | remote(7) | fedora-38(7) | root(7) | host(7) | boltdb(7) |

comments.body:
On first look this could be an race between nc -l actually listening and we connecting. But the test is older so if we only now start to notice a flake maybe something else changed.
So far all seem to fail on bridge mode and on the first port iteration, if it would be a race with `nc -I` I would expect a more random pattern.

I can make a PR to include some debugging commands in there that hopefully give some clues.

[Slightly different symptom](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5050617714966528/html/sys-remote-fedora-38-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--00512):
```
<+015ms> # # podman-remote --url unix:///var/tmp/podman_tmp_ZhgD exec -d 148538bef2df361bd0f2eb07ebde4735a3ee2e0c3fa9cf930309f9fdaa8f0157 nc -l -p 5782 -e /bin/cat
<+093ms> # 083d2dea2cbf3f1f973b494afd66c127cd6e6d4e07236ae4afb97018be76c414
         # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
         # #|     FAIL: ncat got data back (netmode=slirp4netns:port_handler=slirp4netns port=5782)
         # #| expected: 'FXJ2L72d5t'
         # #|   actual: 'Ncat: Connection reset by peer.'
         # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```
Ah thanks, so if slirp4netns is effected as well then I really have to assume the race is between starting nc -l and connecting to it.
